CVE-2016-4074.title: The jv_dump_term function in jq 1.5 allows remote attackers to ca ...
CVE-2016-4074.description: The jv_dump_term function in jq 1.5 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (stack consumption and application crash) via a crafted JSON file. This issue has been fixed in jq 1.6_rc1-r0.

CVE-2025-9403.title: A vulnerability was determined in jqlang jq up to 1.6. Impacted i ...
CVE-2025-9403.description: A vulnerability was determined in jqlang jq up to 1.6. Impacted is the function run_jq_tests of the file jq_test.c of the component JSON Parser. Executing manipulation can lead to reachable assertion. The attack requires local access. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. Other versions might be affected as well.

CVE-2024-23337.title: jq is a command-line JSON processor. In versions up to and includ ...
CVE-2024-23337.description: jq is a command-line JSON processor. In versions up to and including 1.7.1, an integer overflow arises when assigning value using an index of 2147483647, the signed integer limit. This causes a denial of service. Commit de21386681c0df0104a99d9d09db23a9b2a78b1e contains a patch for the issue.

CVE-2024-53427.title: decNumberCopy in decNumber.c in jq through 1.7.1 does not properl ...
CVE-2024-53427.description: decNumberCopy in decNumber.c in jq through 1.7.1 does not properly consider that NaN is interpreted as numeric, which has a resultant stack-based buffer overflow and out-of-bounds write, as demonstrated by use of --slurp with subtraction, such as a filter of .-. when the input has a certain form of digit string with NaN (e.g., "1 NaN123" immediately followed by many more digits).

CVE-2025-48060.title: jq is a command-line JSON processor. In versions up to and includ ...
CVE-2025-48060.description: jq is a command-line JSON processor. In versions up to and including 1.7.1, a heap-buffer-overflow is present in function `jv_string_vfmt` in the jq_fuzz_execute harness from oss-fuzz. This crash happens on file jv.c, line 1456 `void* p = malloc(sz);`. As of time of publication, no patched versions are available.
